description_long: This megaphone features a classic conical design with a wide, flared mouthpiece at one end and a cylindrical handle or body at the other. The mouthpiece is light gray and smoothly transitions into the blue cylindrical section, which likely houses the electronics and battery compartment. The front view reveals a hexagonal speaker grill with a dark border and a central area that may contain slits or vents for sound projection. The overall form is compact and ergonomic, suitable for handheld use in public spaces such as streets, parks, or event venues. The materials appear to be primarily plastic, with some metallic or electronic components likely inside. No visible branding or activation buttons are apparent from the provided views.
description: A compact, blue and gray handheld megaphone with a flared mouthpiece and hexagonal speaker grill, designed for projecting sound in urban environments.
description_view_0: Front view showing the flared gray mouthpiece and the blue cylindrical body behind it.
description_view_1: Side view with only the edge of the flared mouthpiece visible, most of the body is out of frame.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the hexagonal speaker grill with a dark border and central vent details.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the edge of the mouthpiece and part of the body.
attribute_mouthpiece_design: Wide, flared, conical mouthpiece with a smooth, open edge and no visible attachments or coverings.
attribute_speaker_grill_pattern: Hexagonal grill with a dark border and central vent-like details.
